chunks.text: In just thirty years, technology has entirely changed our daily life.
Electric device are beside us from the moment we wake until we sleep.
In the morning, the alarm rings and we check the news on a smartphone.
We pay for breakfast by electronic payment and are guided to the office by the navigation of our automobile.
At the office, equipment has been installed to handle vast amounts of data.
Meetings are held remotely, and documents are signed electronically.
The electrification of vehicle is expected to eliminate exhaust gas and make city air considerably cleaner.
Battery technology is being enhanced at a rapid pace.
the problem of privacy exposure and the digital divide are growing more serious.
To distribute the benefit of technology fairly has become an obligation of society.
Technology is something that enables, not something that rules.
or are dominated by it — that choice is always in our hands.
